issues.author: shakeebshaan
issues.reviewReason: RAN THE ENTIRE ONBOARDING MEAL-BUILD end-to-end as you asked (drove the diet builder's coach-build → prefs sheet → build against the REAL backend, mimicking a user pausing on the prefs questions). RESULT: it builds correctly and honors prefs (picked North-Indian → got 'Chicken Roti Roll' w/ roti/curd/ghee, macros on-target) — but it took ~47s (was ~60s+). I improved it: the enriched warm was running slots SEQUENTIALLY (prefetchMealIdeas, ~4×15s) so it never finished during the prefs window — now warms all slots CONCURRENTLY; and build-all asked for count:20 which fires a 2nd LLM batch — dropped to count:8 single-batch (count isn't in the cache key, build only uses the top idea). 60s+ → ~47s (22ba43f6). HONEST BLOCKER: <3s is NOT reachable from the frontend. Each meal slot is a live /ai/suggest-meal LLM call (~15-45s) PLUS a per-idea image lookup; 4 slots can't be hidden behind a ~20-30s prefs window no matter how I warm. True <3s needs a BACKEND change — either (a) pre-generate/cache meal decks per (diet_type, goal, prefs) so the build is a cache read, or (b) an INSTANT template first-fill (fast_plan_assembler-style, like onboarding finalize already does) that the LLM refines in the background. Both are backend work / a product call — your decision. I did not mark this resolved. The frontend is now at its floor; the wait does at least happen on the blurred screen (987b) and is ~13s shorter.
